framework.name: Model AI Governance Framework for Generative AI
framework.issuing_body: Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) and AI Verify Foundation
framework.scope: A voluntary, sector-agnostic governance framework for organisations developing, deploying, or using generative AI systems. It sets out nine dimensions to be considered in totality so stakeholders can manage GenAI risks while supporting innovation, with emphasis on explainability, human oversight, accountability, testing, security, provenance, and data protection.
framework.applies_to: Private-sector organisations and other stakeholders developing, deploying, or using generative AI in Singapore; sector- and technology-agnostic; relevant across organization sizes where GenAI is used, including model developers, deployers, policymakers, research community, and end users.
framework.penalties: No legal enforcement mechanism or statutory penalties are stated; the framework is voluntary and non-binding guidance from IMDA and AI Verify Foundation.

control.statement: Ensure users can understand the capabilities and limitations of the generative AI system and know when they are interacting with AI-generated content.
Maintain human oversight over generative AI systems, especially for high-risk use cases, with defined checkpoints for human intervention.
Assign clear accountability for the development, deployment, and use of generative AI systems.
Establish data, model, and system governance practices to manage risks throughout the AI lifecycle.
Test and evaluate generative AI systems before deployment and periodically thereafter to confirm they are safe, robust, and fit for purpose.
Implement mechanisms to report, respond to, and learn from incidents involving generative AI systems.
Put appropriate security measures in place to protect generative AI systems and related assets from malicious attacks and misuse.
Enable users to identify AI-generated content and its origin, where feasible, through provenance mechanisms.
Respect data protection obligations and intellectual property rights when building and operating generative AI systems.

source.title: PDPC Model AI Governance Framework page
